description: Letter probably from the leaders of the Jewish community in Alexandria to Efrayim b. Shemarya in Fusṭāṭ (fl. 1007–1055 CE), describing the ransoming and generous treatment of two Jewish captives by Netanʾel ha-Kohen, and the special prayers recited in honor of both Netanʾel and David b. Yiṣḥaq ha-Levi on every Sabbath on account of their past generosity. The letter also begins to narrate the tale of a ship belonging to the Muslim pirate Jabbāra b. Mukhtār. The ship set sail from Anṭalya (western Anatolia) heading west, but called in at Ramāda (Ifrīqiya) with ten Jewish captives whose money had been stolen and who needed to be ransomed. Dating: 11th century. (Information from CUDL.) This document has been cited extensively in scholarship. The sender is the Alexandrian judge Yeshuʿa b. Yosef according to Frenkel. See Bodl. MS heb. a 3/28 (PGPID 19284) and related documents listed there.
description_he: מכתב ממנהיגי הקהילה היהודית באלכסנדריה לאפרים בן שמריה בפוסטאט. פדיון שבויים יהודים וסיפור ספינת שודדי הים ג'בארה.
